messages.text: Wrapped up the API rate-limiter PR — tests are green, ready for review. Moving on to the caching layer next.
Blocked on the search indexing task — waiting on the new Elasticsearch cluster to be provisioned. @ops any ETA?
Decision: We're going with Redis for session caching instead of Memcached. Better persistence options and we already have the infrastructure.
Shipped the onboarding email flow to staging. Need someone to QA the templates before we push to prod.
Quick update: caching layer is trickier than expected. Found some edge cases with TTL invalidation. Might need an extra day.
Redis caching PR is up. Also started looking into the webhook retry logic — current implementation drops events silently.
Elasticsearch cluster is live! Unblocked on search indexing. Starting the migration script today.
Onboarding emails QA'd and approved. Pushing to production this afternoon.
Resolved the TTL edge cases. Caching layer is working well now — 40% reduction in DB read latency in staging.
Decision: Adding exponential backoff to webhook retries with a dead letter queue for failed events. Will document the approach in Notion.
Finished the user research synthesis for the dashboard redesign. Key insight: users want fewer clicks to reach their top metrics.
Working on the Q1 roadmap draft. Need input from eng on feasibility for the analytics overhaul — can we scope a spike?
Blocked: The A/B test for the new pricing page can't launch until legal reviews the updated terms. Following up today.
Decision: Dashboard redesign will be phased. Phase 1 focuses on the metrics overview; Phase 2 adds customizable widgets.
Q1 roadmap draft shared in Notion. Requesting async feedback by end of week.
Legal approved the pricing page terms! A/B test is unblocked — launching tomorrow.
Phase 1 wireframes for dashboard redesign are in Figma. Would love eyes on them during this week's design review.
Completed the vendor renewal analysis for Q1. Three contracts up — recommending we consolidate from two monitoring tools to one.
Onboarding automation for new hires is 80% done. Slack channel creation and Google Workspace provisioning are automated. Still need to add Notion access.
Decision: Cancelling the DataDog contract in favor of Grafana Cloud. Saves $1,200/month and the team prefers the dashboards.
Blocked on Notion API access for the onboarding flow — need admin approval to create an integration token.
Got Notion admin access. Onboarding automation is fully working now — tested with a dummy account end-to-end.
DataDog migration plan documented. Grafana Cloud setup starts next week. Current dashboards will be recreated by Friday.
Shipped the deprovisioning workflow too — when someone's last day is marked in BambooHR, it triggers access revocation across all tools within 24 hours.
Search indexing migration is 60% complete. Processing about 2M records — should finish by tomorrow morning.
Caching layer merged to main. Starting on the API versioning task now.
Onboarding emails are live in production! Monitoring delivery rates — 98.5% so far.
Webhook retry system is in review. Dead letter queue is tested and working. Need Alex to review the backoff logic.
Blocked: Hit a memory issue with the bulk indexing job. Need to batch the records — working on a fix.
Pricing page A/B test is live! Control vs. simplified layout. Running for 2 weeks, targeting 5k visitors per variant.
Got good async feedback on the roadmap. Incorporating suggestions and will finalize by Friday.
Design review went well. Minor tweaks to the dashboard wireframes — updating Figma today.
Q1 tool spend report is ready. Total SaaS spend: $14,200/month across 23 tools. Identified 4 tools with overlapping functionality.
Wrote up the onboarding/offboarding automation docs in Notion. New hires and managers can now self-serve the checklist.
Decision: Scheduling a tool audit review meeting for next Wednesday. Goal is to cut 2-3 redundant subscriptions by end of Q1.
Fixed the memory issue — batching records in chunks of 50k. Migration resumed and looking good.
API versioning design doc is up. Proposing URL-based versioning (v1, v2) with 6-month deprecation windows.
Webhook retry PR approved and merged. Dead letter queue dashboard is live — already caught 3 events that would have been lost.
Starting on the password reset flow redesign. Current flow has a 12% drop-off rate — aiming to cut that in half.
Q1 roadmap finalized and shared company-wide. Three themes: performance, onboarding, and analytics.
Early pricing test data: simplified layout showing 8% higher click-through to signup. Still early but promising.
Dashboard wireframes finalized. Handing off to eng for Phase 1 implementation estimate.
Two new hires starting Monday — onboarding automation triggered successfully for both. All accounts provisioned.
Grafana Cloud setup is complete. Migrated 8 of 12 DataDog dashboards. Remaining 4 by tomorrow.
Decision: Standardizing all internal docs on Notion. Moving remaining Google Docs and Confluence pages over the next 2 weeks.
Search indexing migration complete! All 2.1M records indexed. Query latency dropped from 800ms to 120ms.
API versioning implementation started. V2 endpoints will coexist with V1 — no breaking changes for existing clients.
Starting on the notification service refactor. Current system sends duplicate notifications — fixing the deduplication logic.
Password reset redesign is in review. Reduced steps from 4 to 2. Added magic link option as an alternative.
Eng estimated Phase 1 dashboard at 3 weeks. We're good to slot it into Sprint 7.
Pricing A/B test at day 3: simplified layout at 9.2% CTR vs. 7.8% control. Statistical significance at 82% — need more data.
Kicking off the analytics overhaul spike next week. Alex and Priya will lead the technical assessment.
All DataDog dashboards migrated to Grafana Cloud. DataDog cancellation submitted — effective end of month.
Started the Confluence-to-Notion migration. 45 pages identified — prioritizing active project docs first.
Weekly ops summary: 2 new hires onboarded, DataDog sunset complete, tool audit meeting scheduled. Team is in good shape going into next week.
